blurb:
Otis was on the county shelter's kill list just because his number came up. When the rescue team found him, he was so stressed in that loud, overcrowded place that he was urinating on himself. The second they got him into a quiet room, he buried his head in someone's lap and didn't want to let go. He just needed out.

Now that he's had time to decompress, Otis has turned back into the dog he always was. Fun-loving, attention-seeking, eager to please. He remembered his house training right away and how to sit politely. All that good behavior was still there, just buried under pure stress. The volunteers say he's the kind of dog who will tail-wag his way through your entire day.

Whoever adopts Otis is getting a loyal companion who knows what it's like to need someone. He's not going to take a good home for granted. Our Pal's Place is in Marietta, and you can submit an inquiry through their website to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Dramatic rescue from kill list creates powerful emotional connection with readers
• Well-behaved house-trained dog appeals to wide range of potential adopters
• Transformation story from stressed shelter dog to loving companion shows resilience

blurb:
If you want a dog with personality, Goober is your guy. He's a chocolate lab who does a Chewbacca impression and likes to sing along to things. He's also a little wary of loud noises and new people at first, but give him a few treats and he turns into a social butterfly.

Goober loves his rope toys and giving kisses, and apparently he's got quite the singing voice. The volunteers say he's goofy in all the best ways. He's not going to be a calm, reserved dog. He's going to have opinions about things and he's going to let you know.

If you're looking for a dog who will make you laugh and keep things interesting, Goober is going to fit right in. Our Pal's Place is in Marietta, and you can submit an inquiry through their website to meet this guy and hear his Chewbacca impression for yourself.
scoring_notes:
• Chewbacca impression and singing abilities create memorable and shareable content
• Fun personality appeals to families wanting entertaining and interactive pet
• Limited backstory suggests shorter shelter stay with less urgency than other candidates

blurb:
Chessie is a fantastic cat with one complication. She lived in a home for a year but was returned because she would sometimes bite without warning. Her previous family said she was sweet and loving otherwise, but that's obviously a deal-breaker for most people.

Here's what she is when she's not stressed: confident, friendly, loves head butts and kisses. She gets excited playing with wand toys and will steal them from you and run away with them. She purrs beautifully and makes biscuits like a champion. She doesn't love being picked up but tolerates being held if you support her back legs. She wants to be queen cat, so she'd do best as an only cat.

Chessie needs experienced cat people who won't be her first rodeo. No young kids. The biting might not happen in a different environment with people who understand cats better, but there's no guarantee. She's part of Good Mews' Kitty Keeper program, which means they'll work with you long-term if issues come up.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet Chessie. Call or email to get the process started.
shelter_hours: Hours not specified - contact for details
scoring_notes:
• High-needs case with biting history creates compelling underdog story for experienced adopters
• Shelter's Kitty Keeper program shows long-term commitment and support for adopters
• Returned after year-long placement suggests very extended shelter stay and urgent need

blurb:
His name is Pork Tenderloin, and yes, you have to call him that. Someone at the shelter had a sense of humor, and now this handsome guy is stuck with it. He doesn't seem to mind.

He's sweet but particular about when he wants attention. He'll seek you out when he's in the mood for affection, which is honestly the best kind of cat behavior. No begging, no neediness, just a cat who knows what he wants and when he wants it. He can get overwhelmed more quickly than some cats, so he needs someone who gets feline body language and respects his boundaries.

Pork Tenderloin needs a calm environment and a patient approach. If you've had cats before and understand that sometimes they need space, he has the potential to form a really special bond with you. He just needs someone cat-savvy who won't push too hard too fast.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet Pork Tenderloin. Call or email to get the process started.
shelter_hours: Hours not specified - contact for details
scoring_notes:
• Hilarious name will grab reader attention and generate social media buzz
• Perfect match for experienced cat owners who appreciate independent feline personalities
• Requires specific match but limited urgency indicators in shelter timeline

blurb:
Junior's people suddenly decided they were allergic to him after years together. That was their story anyway. So off to the county shelter he went, where people kept walking past his kennel. Black dog syndrome is real, and Junior felt every bit of it.

But here's what makes Junior special: he has a standing ice cream date. His favorite volunteer Katie takes him to Bruster's and Pelican's regularly, and he absolutely loves these outings. After ice cream, he sometimes goes home with Katie for sleepovers, and she says he's perfectly behaved. House trained, well mannered, just a good dog who wants to be someone's buddy again.

Junior does best as the only dog and prefers older kids, but if you're looking for a loyal companion who will never take you for granted, this is your guy. He knows what it's like to be abandoned and he won't forget the person who gives him a real home. Our Pal's Place is in Marietta, and you can inquire about Junior through their website.
scoring_notes:
• Ice cream dates with volunteer create charming hook that readers will remember and share
• Black dog syndrome and abandonment story highlights overlooked pets needing advocates
• Multiple rejections and extended volunteer relationships suggest long shelter stay

blurb:
Beignet has been through this before. She was adopted from Good Mews as a kitten, but somehow ended up scared and alone at a county shelter. The shelter called Good Mews, and now she's back where people know her name and want her to succeed.

She went straight into foster care to rebuild her confidence, and it worked. She's social when she eats, loves being petted, and when she wants your attention, she'll sit close or lean against you. She can give gentle love bites when she gets overstimulated, so you'll want to watch her signals and give her space when she needs it.

Beignet deserves someone who gets that she's been let down before and wants to do right by her this time. She's sweet and affectionate, just needs an owner who understands cats and won't take the occasional nip personally.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet Beignet. Call or email to get the process started.
shelter_hours: Hours not specified - contact for details
scoring_notes:
• Heartbreaking comeback story of cat who was abandoned after being adopted as kitten
• Sweet and affectionate personality with clear behavioral guidelines for potential adopters
• Multiple shelter stays indicate extended wait time and urgent need for forever home
